reason: Both answers hit the core points: drop "AI-powered," reposition on the forecasting outcome, narrow the category, shift the buyer/competitive set, and demote PM to supporting context. Answer A adds a useful risk flag about verifying retention drivers before committing to the narrative. Answer B adds concrete named competitors (Float, Runn, Parative-style), a P&L/margin framing tying utilization to CFO-level value, and a crisp litmus test for success. B's additions are more concretely actionable and it is written more clearly, whereas A's heavy shorthand reduces readability without adding equivalent substance. Neither has factual errors; the difference is modest.
